Patch Set 4:

Reviewers, could you please review this patch? I just rebased and retested it for many times to make it pass Maloo tests.
Patch Set 4: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 4:

Niu, since this is a user-space patch, do you think using strtod() directly in patch set 2 is easier than the curren one?

Patch Set 4:

I think the complexity of using strtod() or parsing the numbers before and after '.' separately are in same level, and I prefer to not using the 'double' variables.
Patch Set 4: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 4: Code-Review-1

(5 comments)

The other reason to avoid strtod() is that it considers "P" or "p" in the number to mean "power of two" according to the man page, like "E" or "e" means "power of ten", which would break the whole reason for this patch - namely that the user wants to specify "petabyte" values with a "P" suffix. 

-1 because the fractions need to explicitly use base 10 to avoid strange errors.
Uploaded patch set 5.

Patch Set 5:

Looks good to me.

parents.subject: LU-6401 uapi: change uapi_kernelcomm.h to LGPLv2.1 license

commit.subject: LU-7506 lfs: "lfs quota -h" should support petabytes output
commit.message:
LU-7506 lfs: "lfs quota -h" should support petabytes output

This patch adds human-readable petabytes output and allows to set
fraction block limit, e.g. 1.5P.
Also, it adds sanity-quota.sh test_27d to verify these new functions,
and replaces some sprintf() with snprintf() to meet the code style
requirement.

Signed-off-by: Emoly Liu <emoly.liu@intel.com>
Change-Id: I1dced352687e8e53988f85d2781396e647b62865
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/17441
Tested-by: Jenkins
